title: misconception_library_entry
description: One game's full misconception catalog. Contains one entry per error category documented for this game and concept. Used by Misconception Architect, Loop Purity Auditor, Teacher Evidence Dashboard, and clean-replay design. See docs/os_spec_2026.md Section 4.1.

game_family.description: Interaction family from the OS taxonomy. e.g. 'combine_and_build'.

concept.description: The specific mathematical concept this game teaches. e.g. 'additive decomposition'.

math_domain.description: Broad math domain. e.g. 'Number and Operations — Addition'.

misconceptions.description: All documented misconceptions for this game. Should cover all six required error categories (procedure_slip, concept_confusion, representation_mismatch, impulsive_guess, rule_misunderstanding, strategic_overload).

id.description: Unique slug within this entry. e.g. 'bakery_impulsive_tap'.

category.description: Which of the six required error categories this misconception belongs to.

label.description: Short human-readable name for this misconception. e.g. 'Tap before tracking'.

description.description: What the learner believes or does wrong. State the misunderstanding, not just the behavior.

likely_cause.description: Why a learner develops this error. Cognitive or instructional root, not just 'they didn't know'.

how_it_appears_in_play.description: What an observer would see. Specific, behavioral, distinguishable from other error categories.

detection_signal.description: What the game can measure or observe to classify this error. Should be implementable without camera or audio. e.g. 'tap interval < 400ms on 3+ consecutive items'.

best_feedback_response.description: The most effective immediate response to this error. Must name what to notice or do differently — not just 'try again'.

best_clean_replay_task.description: A simpler version of the challenge that isolates the exact misunderstanding. Should be achievable in under 60 seconds.

reflection_prompt.description: One short question to ask the learner after this error repeats twice or more. Tied to the planning/monitoring/evaluation framework.
